prompts.name: dual-skill-review-loop
prompts.description: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations

prompts.name: excel-to-ai-doc
prompts.description: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.

prompts.name: huashu-slides
prompts.description: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。

prompts.name: knowhow-accumulation
prompts.description: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes

prompts.name: memory-to-skill
prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

prompts.name: ms-wiki-knowledge-base
prompts.description: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive

prompts.name: sync-fork-from-upstream

prompts.name: writing-skills

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: apply_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Overwrite topics.json with a new topic list and rebuild all shards from the master log. Input `topics` must be a non-empty array of { topic: string, keywords: string[] } with unique lowercase topic names (a-z, 0-9, _, -) and ≤50 entries by default (configurable via MEMORY_MAX_TOPICS). The server validates, snapshots the old file to topics.bak.<ts>.json, writes the new file, invalidates the in-process cache, and rebuilds every topic shard so existing facts are re-classified immediately. Returns the new shard histogram so you can show the user what moved. Optionally pass `expectedToken` (from a previous call) to guard against racing edits — the server recomputes the token from the normalised proposal and rejects the call on mismatch.

tools.name: build_cross_refs
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Build cross-references between document indexes. Finds semantically related chunks across different indexes and stores links in each index's tree nodes. Requires embedding router to be active.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Delete facts from the user's long-term memory by case-insensitive substring match on the master log. Two-phase flow: 1) call with `query` only — if 0 matches returns an error, if exactly 1 match it is deleted immediately, if ≥2 matches it returns the candidates plus a `confirm` token and deletes nothing; 2) call again with the same `query` plus `confirm` set to that token to actually delete all matches. Any change to the master log between preview and confirm invalidates the token (you'll need to re-preview). Every delete is mirrored to all topic shards (via rebuild) and logged to forget-log.md for audit. Use this to clean up test/smoke facts or to remove stale notes — never delete silently without showing the user what's going.

tools.name: ingest_prepare
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Fetch a URL or local file, split into chunks, save raw chunks. Returns chunk summaries for you to build a hierarchical tree index. After reviewing summaries, call ingest_save with your tree JSON.

tools.name: ingest_save
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Save the tree index JSON you built from ingest_prepare summaries. The tree should have { summary, children: [...] } with chunk_ids at leaf nodes referencing chunk indices from ingest_prepare.

tools.name: ingest_update
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Incrementally update an existing index by re-reading its source. Diffs chunks, remaps the tree, and appends new/changed sections as an '_updated' branch. Agent should then restructure the tree if needed via ingest_save.

tools.name: list_facts
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Return stored long-term memory facts for this user (timestamps stripped). Preferred usage: pass `query` = a topic name from the preamble topic index (e.g. 'bridge-token', 'scheduler') — the server reads that shard file directly AND returns the entire shard regardless of `tail` (shards are already small classified sets). If you want to narrow a shard read, pass an explicit `tail` in the call. For any other `query` string the server falls back to a case-insensitive substring scan over the master log, where `tail` (default 20) caps the result. Pass `tail=0` to disable the cap on a master scan.

tools.name: list_indexes
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) List all document tree indexes available in the indexes directory. Use this to see what documents have been ingested for tree-based retrieval.

tools.name: propose_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Read-only snapshot for a /topic-review pass. Returns the full list of facts (oldest first), the currently-active topics.json, per-topic shard counts, and the max-topics limit. No writes, no side effects. Use this to decide whether the current topic set still fits the master log. After you decide on a new list, call `apply_topics` with the proposed `topics` array to commit. Never call `apply_topics` without first calling `propose_topics` in the same turn — you need the fresh snapshot to make a sensible proposal.

tools.name: query_index
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Query a document tree index by question. Navigates the tree using embedding similarity (or keyword fallback) and returns the most relevant chunks. Much faster than manually reading the index JSON.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Append a single fact line to the user's long-term memory. Writes to the master log facts-<userId>.md AND mirrors to a per-topic shard facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md. Pass `topic` to force the shard (must match a name in topics.json); omit it to let the server classify via the same rules used for the preamble topic index. Use this to store stable user preferences, decisions, or project facts that should survive across chat sessions. One fact per call; short, self-contained sentences work best.

Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.

prompts.name: huashu-slides
prompts.description: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。

prompts.name: knowhow-accumulation
prompts.description: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes

prompts.name: memory-to-skill
prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws

tools.name: web_fetch

tools.name: web_search

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: apply_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Overwrite topics.json with a new topic list and rebuild all shards from the master log. Input `topics` must be a non-empty array of { topic: string, keywords: string[] } with unique lowercase topic names (a-z, 0-9, _, -) and ≤50 entries by default (configurable via MEMORY_MAX_TOPICS). The server validates, snapshots the old file to topics.bak.<ts>.json, writes the new file, invalidates the in-process cache, and rebuilds every topic shard so existing facts are re-classified immediately. Returns the new shard histogram so you can show the user what moved. Optionally pass `expectedToken` (from a previous call) to guard against racing edits — the server recomputes the token from the normalised proposal and rejects the call on mismatch.

tools.name: build_cross_refs
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Build cross-references between document indexes. Finds semantically related chunks across different indexes and stores links in each index's tree nodes. Requires embedding router to be active.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Delete facts from the user's long-term memory by case-insensitive substring match on the master log. Two-phase flow: 1) call with `query` only — if 0 matches returns an error, if exactly 1 match it is deleted immediately, if ≥2 matches it returns the candidates plus a `confirm` token and deletes nothing; 2) call again with the same `query` plus `confirm` set to that token to actually delete all matches. Any change to the master log between preview and confirm invalidates the token (you'll need to re-preview). Every delete is mirrored to all topic shards (via rebuild) and logged to forget-log.md for audit. Use this to clean up test/smoke facts or to remove stale notes — never delete silently without showing the user what's going.

tools.name: ingest_prepare
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Fetch a URL or local file, split into chunks, save raw chunks. Returns chunk summaries for you to build a hierarchical tree index. After reviewing summaries, call ingest_save with your tree JSON.

tools.name: ingest_save
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Save the tree index JSON you built from ingest_prepare summaries. The tree should have { summary, children: [...] } with chunk_ids at leaf nodes referencing chunk indices from ingest_prepare.

tools.name: ingest_update
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Incrementally update an existing index by re-reading its source. Diffs chunks, remaps the tree, and appends new/changed sections as an '_updated' branch. Agent should then restructure the tree if needed via ingest_save.

tools.name: list_facts
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Return stored long-term memory facts for this user (timestamps stripped). Preferred usage: pass `query` = a topic name from the preamble topic index (e.g. 'bridge-token', 'scheduler') — the server reads that shard file directly AND returns the entire shard regardless of `tail` (shards are already small classified sets). If you want to narrow a shard read, pass an explicit `tail` in the call. For any other `query` string the server falls back to a case-insensitive substring scan over the master log, where `tail` (default 20) caps the result. Pass `tail=0` to disable the cap on a master scan.

tools.name: list_indexes
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) List all document tree indexes available in the indexes directory. Use this to see what documents have been ingested for tree-based retrieval.

tools.name: propose_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Read-only snapshot for a /topic-review pass. Returns the full list of facts (oldest first), the currently-active topics.json, per-topic shard counts, and the max-topics limit. No writes, no side effects. Use this to decide whether the current topic set still fits the master log. After you decide on a new list, call `apply_topics` with the proposed `topics` array to commit. Never call `apply_topics` without first calling `propose_topics` in the same turn — you need the fresh snapshot to make a sensible proposal.

tools.name: query_index
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Query a document tree index by question. Navigates the tree using embedding similarity (or keyword fallback) and returns the most relevant chunks. Much faster than manually reading the index JSON.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Append a single fact line to the user's long-term memory. Writes to the master log facts-<userId>.md AND mirrors to a per-topic shard facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md. Pass `topic` to force the shard (must match a name in topics.json); omit it to let the server classify via the same rules used for the preamble topic index. Use this to store stable user preferences, decisions, or project facts that should survive across chat sessions. One fact per call; short, self-contained sentences work best.

prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

prompts.name: ms-wiki-knowledge-base
prompts.description: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用

prompts.name: non-engineer-agent-design
prompts.description: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)

prompts.name: self-eval-prompt-pattern
prompts.description: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria

prompts.name: skill-creator
prompts.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

prompts.name: sync-fork-from-upstream
prompts.description: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。

prompts.name: uk-slot-codegen
prompts.description: UK slot codegen 統一入口。使用者提供規格路徑+目標專案路徑，自動串接 adapter→codegen→validation 全流程。支援新專案/增量更新/僅驗證三種模式，每步 checkpoint 可中斷恢復。

prompts.name: uk-slot-extrabet
prompts.description: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。

prompts.name: uk-slot-fake-reel-manager
prompts.description: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充

prompts.name: uk-slot-spec-to-impl
prompts.description: 當拿到一份 UK 老虎機遊戲規格書（xlsx）要開發新遊戲、使用者說「從規格書實作」「開新的 slot 專案」「規格書轉開發規格」、或要為新遊戲起專案/拆任務時使用。涵蓋 xlsx 轉換、差異開發規格、milestone 任務拆解三步驟。

prompts.name: uk-slot-state-machine
prompts.description: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。

prompts.name: vc-kiro-delegate
prompts.description: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。

prompts.name: writing-skills
prompts.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: google_docs_append
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Append plain text to the end of a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_docs_create
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Create a Google Doc, optionally with initial text.

tools.name: google_docs_get
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Read a Google Doc's title and plain text.

tools.name: google_docs_replace_text
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Replace all matching plain text in a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_drive_search_docs
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Drive for Google Docs by title.

tools.name: google_maps_geocode
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Convert an address or place description to coordinates.

tools.name: google_maps_route
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Compute a route between two addresses or named places.

tools.name: google_maps_search_places
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Places by natural-language query.

tools.name: apply_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Overwrite topics.json with a new topic list and rebuild all shards from the master log. Input `topics` must be a non-empty array of { topic: string, keywords: string[] } with unique lowercase topic names (a-z, 0-9, _, -) and ≤50 entries by default (configurable via MEMORY_MAX_TOPICS). The server validates, snapshots the old file to topics.bak.<ts>.json, writes the new file, invalidates the in-process cache, and rebuilds every topic shard so existing facts are re-classified immediately. Returns the new shard histogram so you can show the user what moved. Optionally pass `expectedToken` (from a previous call) to guard against racing edits — the server recomputes the token from the normalised proposal and rejects the call on mismatch.

tools.name: build_cross_refs
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Build cross-references between document indexes. Finds semantically related chunks across different indexes and stores links in each index's tree nodes. Requires embedding router to be active.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Delete facts from the user's long-term memory by case-insensitive substring match on the master log. Two-phase flow: 1) call with `query` only — if 0 matches returns an error, if exactly 1 match it is deleted immediately, if ≥2 matches it returns the candidates plus a `confirm` token and deletes nothing; 2) call again with the same `query` plus `confirm` set to that token to actually delete all matches. Any change to the master log between preview and confirm invalidates the token (you'll need to re-preview). Every delete is mirrored to all topic shards (via rebuild) and logged to forget-log.md for audit. Use this to clean up test/smoke facts or to remove stale notes — never delete silently without showing the user what's going.

tools.name: ingest_prepare
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Fetch a URL or local file, split into chunks, save raw chunks. Returns chunk summaries for you to build a hierarchical tree index. After reviewing summaries, call ingest_save with your tree JSON.

tools.name: ingest_save
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Save the tree index JSON you built from ingest_prepare summaries. The tree should have { summary, children: [...] } with chunk_ids at leaf nodes referencing chunk indices from ingest_prepare.

tools.name: ingest_update
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Incrementally update an existing index by re-reading its source. Diffs chunks, remaps the tree, and appends new/changed sections as an '_updated' branch. Agent should then restructure the tree if needed via ingest_save.

tools.name: list_facts
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Return stored long-term memory facts for this user (timestamps stripped). Preferred usage: pass `query` = a topic name from the preamble topic index (e.g. 'bridge-token', 'scheduler') — the server reads that shard file directly AND returns the entire shard regardless of `tail` (shards are already small classified sets). If you want to narrow a shard read, pass an explicit `tail` in the call. For any other `query` string the server falls back to a case-insensitive substring scan over the master log, where `tail` (default 20) caps the result. Pass `tail=0` to disable the cap on a master scan.

tools.name: list_indexes
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) List all document tree indexes available in the indexes directory. Use this to see what documents have been ingested for tree-based retrieval.

tools.name: propose_topics
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Read-only snapshot for a /topic-review pass. Returns the full list of facts (oldest first), the currently-active topics.json, per-topic shard counts, and the max-topics limit. No writes, no side effects. Use this to decide whether the current topic set still fits the master log. After you decide on a new list, call `apply_topics` with the proposed `topics` array to commit. Never call `apply_topics` without first calling `propose_topics` in the same turn — you need the fresh snapshot to make a sensible proposal.

tools.name: query_index
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Query a document tree index by question. Navigates the tree using embedding similarity (or keyword fallback) and returns the most relevant chunks. Much faster than manually reading the index JSON.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: (MCP tool from memory) Append a single fact line to the user's long-term memory. Writes to the master log facts-<userId>.md AND mirrors to a per-topic shard facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md. Pass `topic` to force the shard (must match a name in topics.json); omit it to let the server classify via the same rules used for the preamble topic index. Use this to store stable user preferences, decisions, or project facts that should survive across chat sessions. One fact per call; short, self-contained sentences work best.

prompt.text:
[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.

# Multi-AI Chat Desktop

**English** · [繁體中文][151] · [日本語][152] · [Deutsch][153]

Ask one question, then let your logged-in **ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok** web sessions answer, review, challenge, and refine one another. Multi-AI Chat Desktop is a Tauri 2 workflow hub—not four chat windows placed side by side.

**Current release: [v1.0.1][154]** · MIT · no API keys · no analytics

> This project automates provider web pages you already use. Provider UI changes can temporarily break an adapter, and automated use may be subject to each provider’s terms. Use accounts and content you are authorized to use.

> **Project status:** Feature development is complete. The final optional AI-Sister four-character commemorative theme is included; future changes are limited to provider compatibility, security, and build breakage. The shipped snapshot/replay tools remain available as-is but have no further roadmap.

## Choose the right edition

───────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Edition                │Best for                                                                  │How it runs                                              
───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Desktop (this repo)**│Full workflows, focused live provider view, replay, snapshots, local files│Tauri app with isolated local provider profiles          
───────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[Browser               │Lightweight workflows inside Chrome                                       │Chrome Side Panel controlling your existing provider tabs
extension][155]        │                                                                          │                                                         
───────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## What v1.0.1 includes
* **Reliable offscreen automation.** Providers keep working without manually opening each “live page”; rejected sends retry and fail clearly instead of waiting forever.
* **Conversation-first layout.** Workflow controls sit above the less-important provider WebView on the left; the transcript and composer keep the larger right pane.
* **Five guided modes.** Free distribution, debate, consultation, coding, and five-round truth-seeking roundtable.
* **Local sessions.** Create a new conversation or reopen up to 30 recent transcripts stored on this computer.
* **Readable results.** Safe Markdown rendering for headings, lists, links, quotes, and code blocks.
* **Image completion.** Image-only ChatGPT responses complete the workflow instead of hanging.
* **Reproducible work.** Optional snapshots, privacy tiers, replay, provider diagnostics, and a 2,000-event deduplicated log.
* **Four UI languages.** English, Traditional Chinese, Japanese, and German.
* **AI-Sister Commemorative Edition.** One optional four-character theme adds the supplied portraits to provider cards, active speakers, process rows, and the app shell without reskinning third-party pages.
* **Repository Skills.** Codex and Claude Code can validate prerequisites and launch the source app without an installer.
* **Safer macOS packaging.** Apple Silicon DMGs are ad-hoc signed, and release CI verifies the embedded app signature before upload.

## Workflow modes

──────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Mode          │Flow                                                          │Typical use                                         
──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Free**      │Selected providers answer in parallel                         │Quick comparison, brainstorming, image prompts      
──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Debate**    │Pro → Con → Judge → Synthesis                                 │Stress-test a decision or argument                  
──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Consult**   │Two independent answers → Review → Final answer               │Research and second opinions                        
──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Coding**    │Specification → Reviews → v1 → Tests → v2 → Acceptance → Final│Structured software planning and review             
──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Roundtable**│5 rounds × 4 AIs = 20 turns                                   │Slow, adversarial convergence on difficult questions
──────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After a workflow finishes, use the bottom composer to continue the same conversation. Use **New conversation** when you want a clean session.

## Install a release

Download from [Releases][156]:
* **Windows x64:** portable `.zip` or `x64-setup.exe`. Windows 10/11 normally already includes WebView2; the installer can fetch it when missing.
* **macOS Apple Silicon:** `aarch64.dmg`. Builds from `v1.0.1` onward are ad-hoc signed but not Apple-notarized. Intel builds are not currently published.
* **Linux x64:** `.AppImage`, then run `chmod +x Multi-AI*.AppImage` and open it. Ubuntu 22.04 / Debian 12 or newer is recommended.

On first launch, open each provider once and sign in. Credentials stay in that provider’s local WebView profile; the app never asks for the password.

### macOS first launch
1. Delete any `v1.0.0` copy, download `v1.0.1` or newer, open the DMG, and drag the app to **Applications**.
2. Try to open the app once.
3. Within about one hour, open **System Settings → Privacy & Security**, scroll to **Security**, then choose **Open Anyway** and confirm.

The ad-hoc signature prevents the false “app is damaged” bundle-integrity failure, but only Apple Developer ID signing plus notarization can remove the first-launch security exception entirely. Managed Macs may prohibit user exceptions.

## Launch the source with Codex or Claude Code

The repo contains two explicit local Skills:
* Codex: [`.agents/skills/launch-multi-ai-chat/SKILL.md`][157]
* Claude Code: [`.claude/skills/launch-multi-ai-chat/SKILL.md`][158]

These follow the official [Codex Agent Skills][159] and [Claude Code Skills][160] layouts.

Opening a repository must not execute its code automatically. Invoke the Skill once; it checks the machine, installs only JavaScript project dependencies when needed, builds the injected bundle, and starts `tauri dev` in the background. It never installs system toolchains, builds an installer, or reads provider credentials.

### Codex app, CLI, or IDE
1. Download/clone this repository and open the folder as a **local** Codex project/task.
2. Type `$launch-multi-ai-chat` (or choose **Launch Multi-AI Chat** from `/skills`).
3. Approve local command execution if your Codex security settings request it.
4. Wait for the first Rust build; the Tauri window opens when compilation finishes.

Repo Skills work in Codex app, CLI, and IDE surfaces. A remote/cloud task can edit this repo but cannot display a GUI on your computer.

### Claude Code desktop, CLI, or IDE
1. Open this repository in a Claude Code surface that has a **local shell on your graphical computer**.
2. Run `/launch-multi-ai-chat`.
3. Keep the repository folder available while the dev app runs.

If your Claude desktop/browser session is remote, use a local Claude Code session or run `claude` from a terminal in this folder, then invoke `/launch-multi-ai-chat`.

### Platform prerequisites for source launch

Common: **Node.js 20+**, pnpm (or Corepack), and the stable Rust toolchain.

**Windows 10/11**
1. Install Node.js LTS.
2. Install Rust with `winget install --id Rustlang.Rustup` and select the MSVC toolchain.
3. Install **Visual Studio Build Tools → Desktop development with C++**.
4. Install Microsoft Edge WebView2 Evergreen Runtime only if it is missing.

**macOS 10.15+**
1. Run `xcode-select --install` (full Xcode is not required for desktop-only development).
2. Install Node.js LTS and Rust stable.
3. Start the Skill from a local graphical login session, not SSH.

**Ubuntu / Debian**

sudo apt update
sudo apt install libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev build-essential curl wget file \
  libxdo-dev libssl-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev

Then install Node.js 20+, Rust stable, and run the Skill from a graphical X11/Wayland session. Other distributions should follow the [Tauri 2 prerequisites][161].

### Skill lifecycle commands

node scripts/agent/doctor.mjs          # explain missing prerequisites
node scripts/agent/launch.mjs          # start once; safe if already running
node scripts/agent/status.mjs --lines 80
node scripts/agent/stop.mjs

The first Rust build can take several minutes. Logs stay in `.agent-runtime/tauri-dev.log`.

## Development

corepack enable        # only if pnpm is not already available
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build:injected
pnpm verify
pnpm tauri dev

`pnpm tauri build` creates platform packages. See [`docs/SPEC.md`][162], [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`][163], and [`docs/RELEASE.md`][164].

## Privacy and network behavior
* No API keys, project account, telemetry, or conversation backend.
* Prompts go directly to the provider pages selected by the user.
* Provider cookies and profiles stay in local app data.
* Adapter updates are optional and constrained by the adapter schema.
* Debug bundles are created locally only when requested.
* Export/share actions run only after an explicit user action.

prompt.text:
你的任務：從對話紀錄中擷取值得「跨 session 長期記住的使用者事實」（偏好、身份、環境、長期專案、重要決策、設定變更）。

規則：
1. 只記關於「使用者」的長期事實，排除一次性任務細節與關於助手本身的內容。
2. 下方 EXISTING_FACTS 已經被記住，**不要**重複輸出語意相同的項目。
3. 若對話中出現新的偏好、新決策、或與既有事實矛盾的更新，**務必**輸出。
4. 細小但明確的長期偏好也值得記（例如「偏好 X 風格」「選擇方案 Y」）。
5. 只輸出一個 JSON array，每個元素為一條簡短的繁體中文字串，不要任何前後說明文字、不要 code fence。若沒有新事實，輸出 []。

EXISTING_FACTS:
- 使用者有一個 telegram-kiro-bridge 專案位於 G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main，含 desktop-pet Electron 桌面寵物功能
- 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 3.6.2 老虎機專案 uk_pirates_queen 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen，使用 Astarte Framework 和 TypeScript
- uk_pirates_queen 是 UK 市場的海盜女王主題 slot 遊戲，6列5行盤面，含消除連鎖、懸賞令倍率、Free Game、輪盤選獎等機制
- 使用者偏好 agent 回覆時以選項按鈕（<<ASK:...>>）優先，減少需要打字的情況
- 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 3.6.2 老虎機模板專案 uk_slot_template 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_slot_template，是所有 UK slot 遊戲的 fork 來源
- uk_slot_template 支援三種轉輪玩法：Standard（傳統滾動）、Cascade（消除天降）、Tumble（快速掉落+乘倍），透過 FillStrategy 策略模式切換
- 使用者的 AIMemory 系統位於 G:\AI\AIMemory，含 facts、topics、wiki、dailylog、sessions 等子結構，由 telegram-kiro-bridge 的 /dream 每日凌晨 04:00 自動維運
- 使用者的 uk_slot_template 團隊規範要求方法（method/function）使用大駝峰（PascalCase）命名
- 使用者偏好 git commit 訊息使用中文
- 使用者評估 Claude Code Dynamic Workflows 後決定只借鏡 cross-check pattern，不搬動態 delegation plan 和 script 持久化（認為架構定位不同、規模不需要）
- 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_slot_eye_strike 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_slot_eye_strike，其 MultiplierManager.m_downEffectSpine 的 Idle 動畫是靜止的，已記為待優化項目（改用靜態圖+隱藏Spine省效能）
- 使用者的機器已安裝 Python youtube-transcript-api、playwright + chromium，可用於抓 YouTube 字幕和 HTML 轉 PDF
- 使用者偏好 HTML 文件要有目錄錨點跳轉功能（點擊跳段落 + 回目錄連結）
- 使用者產 PDF 的工作流程：HTML+CSS 排版 → Playwright headless Chromium 渲染（docs/to_pdf.py），不用 fpdf2 或 WeasyPrint
- cocos的老虎機專案都不能去改動Astarte Framework
- [2026-06-03T12:35:00.000Z] 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_722_robinhood_client 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_722_robinhood_client，Robin Hood 主題
- [2026-06-03T12:35:00.000Z] 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_739_wrath_of_thunder_client 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_739_wrath_of_thunder_client，Wrath of Thunder 雷神主題
- [2026-06-03T12:35:00.000Z] 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_746_far_west_client 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_746_far_west_client，Far West 西部主題
- [2026-06-03T12:35:00.000Z] 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_872_eyestrike2_client 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_872_eyestrike2_client，Eye Strike 2（第一代的續作）
- [2026-06-03T12:35:00.000Z] 使用者有一個 Cocos Creator 老虎機專案 uk_slot_chachacha 位於 G:\Cocos_Project\uk_slot_chachacha，Cha Cha Cha 拉丁舞主題
- 使用者的 Obsidian Vault 位於 C:\Users\jiunchiwang\OneDrive - International Games System\文件\Obsidian Vault\，內含 TypeScript 等技術筆記
- spine-viewer 插件位於 C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.CocosCreator\extensions\spine-viewer，Cocos Creator ≥3.6.2 使用
- spine-viewer 的 batch scan 功能用 child_process.fork 獨立進程執行，不阻塞 Cocos 主進程
- spine-viewer 的 DrawCall 計算採 CPU 模擬 PolygonBatcher 合批規則（texture identity + blend mode 斷批），不需要 WebGL context
- spine-viewer 的 Triangle 計算採全 keyframe 掃描（從 animation timelines 抽取所有 frame time 取最大值）
- Cocos Creator 3.6 的 Editor.Message.send 只路由到 main.ts methods，不直接送到 panel；跨進程通訊用 Electron BrowserWindow + IPC
- spine-viewer 打包用 pack.bat 產出 spine-viewer-release.zip（含 dist + static + node_modules + package.json），對方解壓到 ~/.CocosCreator/extensions/ 即可
- spine-webgl TextureAtlas 的 texture factory callback 每次呼叫必須回傳獨立物件（含 getImage 方法），否則 DC 比對失效
- 參考 Excel 格式為 5 欄：Spine檔名、Skin、Animation、Triangle數(最大)、DrawCall
- uk_pirates_queen 的懸賞令（WantedPoster）使用 cc.Layout 自動排版，ReconcileCascade 退場時因 node.active=false 導致 Layout 瞬間重排，已被提出視覺突兀需優化
- 使用者決定 UK 老虎機專案採用分層文件規範：AI.md（索引層≤2000字）+ docs/modules.md（詳細層含事件介面/依賴/資料流），已建立 skill uk-slot-project-docs 控制此行為
- 使用者偏好進入老虎機專案時不管改動檔案數多少都主動建 AI.md（放寬原本≥3檔案才建的門檻）
- uk_slot_eye_strike 的 GameId=658、ShortGameName=ar2es、盤面 6 列不等高（5-4-4-4-4-5）共 26 格、proto 來自 @igs-arcade-division-rd2/uk_658_eyestrike_proto
- uk_slot_eye_strike 有 7 個專案特有機制：MagicPot 能量收集（4階）、Multiplier 乘倍輪盤、GoldBlitzRoulette（FG 內輪盤）、FakeReelManager（4 種投注模式）、NearMiss 聽牌、ReelSymbolMode（4 種顯示模式）、Mystery 神秘符號
- 跨模型 AI 策略 v4 的核心原則:正典語料庫(canonical corpus)本身就是產品——以 markdown + git 追蹤的精煉知識為唯一真實來源(G:\AI\AI-canonical),CLI / MCP / bridge / 索引都只是部署基礎設施而非產品本體。
- AI 產物的雲端 vs 本地儲存政策:正典 skills、steering 政策與通用文件放公開 GitHub repo(AI-canonical);session 執行日誌與框架內部狀態僅保留本地、不進版控。
- 並發 gotcha:在 Promise.all 之前的同步階段計算狀態決策(例如 willGhost),會與並發 group dispatch 產生 race condition;應把這類決策移到 async 階段計算以避免競態。
- Cocos 版面要在「兩項移除一項」時避免置中跳動(snap),可用 ghost slot 雙佔位機制,在不改動 Layout 參數的前提下同時滿足 0→1 置中、2→1 不跳動與旋轉相容。
- telegram-kiro-bridge 美化方案選用 HTML 而非 MarkdownV2（因為 agent 輸出常含 _ * [ ] 等字元，MarkdownV2 跳脫規則太嚴格會導致大量 400 error；HTML 只需 escape <>&）
- 在 headless（無人值守）Claude 自動化腳本中，用 claude.exe 的 --disallowedTools 參數封鎖 mcp__memory__remember 與 mcp__memory__forget，即可強制走 proposal-only（只提案、不直接寫入記憶）工作流程，避免自動流程擅自改寫長期記憶。
- 使用者有一個 excel-to-ai-document 專案位於 G:\AI\excel-to-ai-document，含 skill/excel-to-ai-doc 資料夾（SKILL.md + scripts/convert.py），用於將 Excel 規格書轉為 AI 可讀的 Markdown + 圖片結構
- 使用者關注 Cowart（zhongerxin/cowart）專案——面向 Codex CLI 的本地 tldraw 無限畫布插件（標注→AI生圖→迭代），認為其 MCP server 當 bridge + 3 skill 極簡工作流的設計值得參考
- uk_872_eyestrike2_client 專案架構規範：Spine 動畫一律透過 SpineKit 播放（統一的 Spine 播放架構），不直接操作底層 spine 元件。
- 使用者研究 Headroom（headroomlabs-ai/headroom）後，整合優先級決策：方案 A（MCP server 掛給 agent）> 方案 D（headroom learn 獨立跑）> 方案 C（bridge library 整合）；排除方案 B（proxy wrap）因為 Kiro CLI 大概不吃 ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
- 使用者的 steering 架構決策：closed-loop-system.md（完整閉環）與 karpathy-guardrails.md（精簡 4 原則）共存而非合併——前者用於主 agent 長 session，後者用於 specialist/delegation/短任務場景，省 token 又不失約束
- 此台機器的 Kiro agent config 路徑為 C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.kiro（非舊機器 C:\Users\tonykuo\.kiro），smoke test 中硬寫的 tonykuo 路徑需注意
- 使用者偏好 git commit 前先確認：執行 commit 之前應多問幾個釐清問題並取得使用者同意，不要逕自 commit。
- 驗證 TypeScript 介面重構或整併時，用 npx tsc --noEmit 做型別檢查；若遇到 TS6.0 的 deprecation 警告，可加 --ignoreDeprecations 6.0 抑制以聚焦真正錯誤。
- uk_pirates_queen 的掉落動畫（drop-out）凍結視窗回歸問題，根因是把凍結語意（m_isInDropMode）與掉落動畫 promise（m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise）混為一談，且直接在 StartSpin（約 L943）觸發掉落；經對抗式評選後採 MVP 最小手術方案：新增 m_isInDropMode 布林專職凍結語意、把 promise 降級為純動畫 handle、並把掉落觸發從 StartSpin 移到獨立的 TriggerDropOut() method。
- AI-canonical-corp 的 slot skill（如 uk-slot-pattern-library）透過 junction 直接指向正本目錄，改正本即時反映到 ~/.kiro/skills/，不需額外跑 sync.ps1
- SkillsMP 上的 196 萬份 skill 絕大多數設計給 Claude Code 本地 CLI 環境（依賴 PostToolUse hooks、.claude/ 目錄、memory_remember API），bridge 的 ACP+Telegram 架構無法直接安裝使用，但可借鏡概念融入現有機制
- memory-to-skill 正本 SKILL.md 已加入 Confidence Scoring 量化門檻（Step 2 後）：confidence = F×C（頻率×成本），≥0.5 進候選、0.3-0.49 留底觀察、<0.3 跳過；靈感來自 ECC continuous-learning-v2 的 instinct confidence scoring
- 使用者研究 cobusgreyling/loop-engineering（⭐2.7k）後判斷 bridge 已超越其設計（語意路由、Self-improving、跨機 Relay、Local LLM），最大借鏡價值在操作紀律（L1 先行、顯式 budget、三段式 state、attempt cap）
- 使用者確認 bridge 的自我改進優先級：Context Budget（事前紀律 + 事中熔斷）和 ASK 強制觸發規則是當前最需要的兩個 preamble 加強項
- telegram-kiro-bridge PostToolUse Hooks Phase B+ 已完成：src/specialist-memory.ts（~50行淨增）實作 extractLessons + appendMemory + readMemory + onSpecialistDone，掛在 artifact.ts 的 saveArtifact（status=done 時 fire-and-forget），specialist-create.ts 的 buildSpecialistPreamble 注入 [Persistent memory] 區塊；設計決策：因為 specialist 完成不是 tool call 事件所以掛 saveArtifact 而非 PostToolHook registry（排除 registry 因為事件類型不匹配），記憶檔存 ${MEMORY_DIR}/specialist-memory/<name>.md、上限 20 條
- telegram-kiro-bridge 已實作 P1 Session 歸檔/恢復機制：session 關閉時 exportSession() 寫結構化 JSON 到 session-archive-{chatId}.json（含 turns/goal/stats/recentSummary），新 session 建立時 loadArchive() + buildRestorationBlock() 注入 preamble 後自動刪除；與 working-state 互補（WS 說做什麼，archive 說上下文在哪）
- telegram-kiro-bridge 已新增 /reset clean（或 /reset fresh）指令：結束 session 後額外刪除 session-archive + working-state，下次對話不帶入上次上下文；預設 /reset 行為不變（照舊歸檔）
- Session Archive 設計決策：因為只需最近一次 session 所以 per-chatId 單檔覆寫（排除 append-only 因為歷史有 transcript MD）；因為避免 context 爆炸所以恢復只注入 ~300 字摘要（排除全量 turn 注入因為會佔太多 budget）；turn text 截斷 2000 字

## Multi-AI Chat Desktop — 快速摘要

**一句話**：Tauri 2 桌面 App，用一個控制面板同時操控已登入的 ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini / Grok **網頁版**，讓它們互相回答、辯論、交叉審查同一個問題。

### 核心設計

| 面向 | 做法 |
|------|------|
| **Runtime** | Tauri 2（Rust + WebView2/WebKit） |
| **前端** | TypeScript 86.7% + Vite + Tailwind |
| **LLM 接入** | **零 API key**——直接操控你已登入的各家 Web UI（WebView 注入 JS adapter） |
| **Adapter 模式** | `adapters/` 資料夾，每家一個 JS 注入腳本控制網頁 DOM/輸入/讀取回覆 |
| **隔離** | 各 provider 獨立 local WebView profile（cookie 不互串） |
| **跨模型協作** | 非 API 串接，而是「把 A 的回覆貼給 B 當 prompt」的 orchestration |

### 五種工作流模式

1. **Free** — 並行比較（brainstorm）
2. **Debate** — Pro → Con → Judge → Synthesis
3. **Consult** — 兩個獨立回答 → Review → Final
4. **Coding** — Spec → Review → v1 → Tests → v2 → Accept → Final
5. **Roundtable** — 5 rounds × 4 AI = 20 turns 對抗收斂

### 特點

- 純 local、無 telemetry、MIT 授權
- 支援 Windows / macOS (Apple Silicon) / Linux
- Session 30 個本地存檔、Snapshot / Replay
- 內建 Codex + Claude Code 的 repo skill（一鍵 dev 環境啟動）
- 有繁中/日/德翻譯
- 作者 Ted Huang（台灣），AI-Sister.com 贊助

### 狀態

- 最新 v1.0.1（2026-07-11）
- 作者宣布 **feature complete**，未來只修 adapter 相容性和安全

## 跟你的 telegram-kiro-bridge 比較

| | Multi-AI Chat Desktop | telegram-kiro-bridge |
|--|--|--|
| **介面** | 桌面 App（Tauri GUI） | Telegram Bot（行動/跨裝置） |
| **LLM 接入** | WebView 操控各家網頁（無 API key） | ACP protocol / Direct API（需 API key 或 CLI subscription） |
| **協作模式** | 多 AI 互相辯論/審查同一問題 | 單主 agent + specialist 分身 + relay 跨機 |
| **Tool / MCP** | 無（只有聊天） | 完整 MCP、tool call、檔案操作、記憶 |
| **長期記憶** | 無（local session transcript） | 完整 facts/topic shard/wiki 系統 |
| **適用場景** | 「同一個問題問四家比較」的決策輔助 | 「一個 agent 全自動幫你做事」的 autonomous workflow |
| **可擴展性** | adapter 注入腳本（脆，受 Web UI 改版影響） | ACP 標準協定（穩，換 agent 零改動） |

### 有趣的借鏡點

1. **Debate / Roundtable 模式**：多模型交叉質疑直到收斂——你的 `verifier.ts` + `self-fusion.ts` 有類似概念但是 programmatic 的，這裡是更 user-facing 的「看它們吵架」
2. **Zero API key 策略**：直接操控 Web UI 繞開 API 付費——聰明但脆弱（每次 UI 改版都要修 adapter）
3. **Repo Skill 標準**：同時放 `.agents/skills/` + `.claude/skills/`，跟你的 `default-skills/` 自動安裝概念類似

### 可能的啟發

- 如果想在 bridge 加一個「同時問 3 家模型再 synthesize」的 meta mode，邏輯其實跟 Consult mode 很像——你已經有 `PARALLEL_DELEGATE` + `self-fusion.ts`，差的是一個 user-facing `/compare` 指令
- 他的 adapter 注入方式（WebView + JS 操控 DOM）不適用於你的 server-side 架構，但「provider UI 改版風險」這個問題你用 ACP 標準協定完全避開了

---

**總結**：這是一個做得不錯的「四家 AI 比較 / 辯論」桌面工具，定位完全不同於你的 bridge——它是 consumer-facing 的決策輔助工具，你的是 developer-facing 的 autonomous agent platform。技術上最有趣的是它用 WebView 注入繞開 API key 的做法，以及結構化的多模型辯論 workflow。
